intro.title: What Is a Vector?
paragraphs: Vectors help us describe movement on a coordinate plane.

segments.value: Every vector has a direction and magnitude (length).
For example, ⟨0, 2⟩ points straight up. Its direction is 90° and its magnitude is 2.

questions.hint: The first number is the horizontal move and the second is the vertical move. ⟨4, 1⟩ means 4 right and 1 up.
questions.explanation: ⟨4, 1⟩ moves 4 units right and 1 unit up, so the tip lands at (4, 1).

questions.hint: A negative first number means moving left. ⟨−2, 3⟩ means 2 left and 3 up.
questions.explanation: ⟨−2, 3⟩ moves 2 units left (negative horizontal) and 3 units up, so the tip lands at (−2, 3).

questions.hint: Follow the arrow from the origin. Count how far it moves left or right, then how far up or down.
questions.explanation: The arrow moves 3 units left and 2 units up from the origin, so it is the vector ⟨−3, 2⟩.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 5 and direction 270°.
questions.hint: Direction 270° points straight down. A magnitude of 5 means the arrow is 5 units long, so move 5 units down.
questions.explanation: 270° points straight down, and magnitude 5 means a length of 5. So the vector is ⟨0, −5⟩: 0 horizontal and 5 down.

questions.hint: The vector is the long side of a right triangle. Its legs are 3 (across) and 4 (up). How long is the slanted side?
questions.explanation: ⟨3, 4⟩ forms a right triangle with legs 3 and 4. The vector is the hypotenuse of a 3-4-5 triangle, so its magnitude is 5.
